_note: PD sub-menu galleries — curated per image, SEPARATELY from project pages (an image only appears here if you put it here). Each item = one image. The view sorts newest-first by year+month. `project` (a slug) links the item to a project page when that project is published; null = standalone asset. Per-image `caption` is the drafted description (editable). Managed by /gk-pd; safe to hand-edit.

prop-design.description: GeneSnip is a lab workstation for a film about stem-cell research and cell duplication — a 2010s DNA-examination interface a technician uses to run a comparison test. It reads a reference strand against a patient's query strand, flags each mismatch, and scores the match: Hamming distance, error rate, percent identity. The comparison is interactive — you can hand-edit the query sequence and it re-scores the result against the reference.

ORION is the film’s near-future search engine — the web on Nora’s laptop. When she looks up the massacre and the riot, the results come back filtered: an overview that explains them away, and a notice that results were “excluded pending verification.” It’s how the state quietly erases what happened. The search, results, and articles are all built and clickable.
